title: CS 494 final project team evaluation (Feb 2024 OSU course form)
html:
TL;DR

  • Feb 16, 2024 — Yahir downloads and saves the CS 494 Final Project Team Evaluation form: 7 Likert-scale criteria (1-4 scale, attendance / contribution / communication / timeliness / code-quality / collaboration / overall-success) plus a 100-point allocation row across teammates.
  • Form is completely blank — no team-member names filled in, no scores assigned, no narrative responses written.
  • 4 narrative questions follow: justification for high/low allocations, overall team-effectiveness, and lessons-learned for future teams.
  • This is a template-archive — Yahir captured the eval-form structure for reference, possibly to draft answers offline. The actual graded submission lived in Canvas.
  • CS 494 is an OSU upper-division CS course (Web Development per OSU catalog) — places Yahir in cs coursework track winter-2024, two terms before the Shanghai program.

Why this matters

  • A clean blank-form artifact — counts as a google docs junk drawer subtype: form-template-saved-but-unfilled.
  • Sits alongside the DEIB phase doc as another "administrative-context-saved-for-reference" exhibit — Yahir building a personal reference-file of forms he might need to revisit.
  • The peer-evaluation framework itself (7 criteria + 100-point allocation) is a legible artifact for any future synthesis on Yahir's exposure to structured assessment frameworks — relevant to his later venture-building (cf. deib phase framework's seedling/budding/blossoming structure).

Connections

osu · cs coursework · 2022 09 15 deib phase information for intern candidates · google docs junk drawer
